title: The LLM Model Pick That Makes or Breaks Hermes & OpenClaw #minimax
summary: Keith AI discusses how the choice of LLM model significantly impacts the performance and viability of the Hermes and OpenClaw projects. The video examines which model selection is decisive for these initiatives.
writeup: A rapid decision-tree for LLM selection drawn from extensive hands-on testing of Hermes and Open Claw, two open-source models occupying a distinct niche from the frontier providers. The speaker maps five concrete use-case buckets to specific model choices, prioritizing cost, reliability, and rate-limit tolerance as the decisive variables. The through-line is that no single model dominates across all axes: quality, cost, agent reliability, coding proficiency, and predictable billing each point to a different winner.

similar.title: New #1 open-source AI model is here!
The insane engineering of Deepseek V4
21 Hermes Lessons to Get You Started

key_points: For the best possible answer regardless of cost, Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini are the top tier; for the absolute cheapest option, DeepSeek is the recommendation.
MiniMax M2.7 ranks as the top budget agent choice for running with minimal headaches, rated above other open alternatives tested.
Kimi is the preferred model for coding tasks on a budget, occupying a specific niche distinct from the general-purpose budget agents.
OpenCode Go offers a fixed $10-per-month billing model that eliminates exploding API costs, though users will encounter rate limits and must tolerate wait times.
Hermes and Open Claw represent a separate use-case category from the frontier and budget tiers covered in the summary, addressed in the fuller video context.
